analysis.note: Predicative nominative neuter singular with idam: “this is mere vijñapti.”

verification.detail: compound stem vijñaptimātra as Napumsaka/Prathama/Eka: pass; relation karmadharaya: unsupported (logged)

analysis.note: In the printed sequence evedam = eva + idam by vowel sandhi.
Subject of the quoted clause, with vijñaptimātram as predicate.
Marks the preceding clause as quoted thought/apprehension.
Explanatory particle: “for”; printed as hy before upalambhataḥ.
Present active participle of upa-√labh, used concessively with api: “even while/for one apprehending.” The noun upalambha would not normally yield the form upalambhataḥ as an a-stem case-form.
Nominative masculine singular present active participle of causative sthāpayati, from √sthā: “placing, setting up.” Stem corrected to sthāpayant for the masculine nominative singular form sthāpayan.
Indeclinable: here taken spatially, “in front / before oneself,” rather than temporally “first.”
Object of sthāpayan: “something, anything.” Stem corrected from masculine kaścit to the neuter indefinite kiñcit.
Locative governed by avatiṣṭhate: “in that-mere,” i.e. in the mere item just referred to, not a Sāṃkhya-style subtle element here.

verification.detail: compound stem tanmātra as Napumsaka/Saptami/Eka: pass; relation karmadharaya: unsupported (logged)

analysis.note: Negates avatiṣṭhate; printed together with it as nāvatiṣṭhate by a + a → ā.
Ātmanepada-form, present 3rd singular of ava-√sthā: “abides, remains, takes a stand.”

justifications.decision: Grammatically, idam is nominative neuter singular and takes vijñaptimātram as a predicative nominative. The final member mātra, used after another noun, has the force “mere/only,” so the compound is not translated as “a measure of information” but as the technical-looking “mere presentation / representation-only.”
options_considered: “this is a measure or quantity of information”
justifications.chosen: vijñaptimātram eva idam = “this is nothing but vijñapti / cognitive presentation.”

evidence.translation: No commentary evidence supplied in this ablation; decision rests on nominative agreement and the lexical force of ifc. mātra.

justifications.decision: The form upalambhataḥ is best explained as genitive singular of an -ant participial stem upalambhat. The dictionary noun upalambha would not give -ataḥ in ordinary a-stem declension. With api it yields the concessive sense: “even while apprehending” or “even for one who apprehends.”
options_considered: genitive singular participle: “of/for one apprehending; while apprehending”
ablative/genitive of noun upalambha: “because of/from apprehension”
justifications.chosen: upalambhataḥ as genitive singular masculine present active participle, concessive with api.

evidence.translation: No commentary evidence supplied; decision rests on the morphology of -ant participles versus a-stem nouns.

justifications.decision: sthāpayan is a nominative masculine singular participle governing the accusative neuter kiñcit. agrataḥ is an indeclinable that can mean either “in front” or “first”; the construction “placing something agrataḥ” favors the spatial sense “before/in front” rather than the temporal “first.”
options_considered: “setting up some doctrine at the beginning”
justifications.chosen: sthāpayann agrataḥ kiñcit = “placing something in front / before oneself.”

evidence.translation: No commentary evidence supplied; decision rests on the transitive participle plus accusative object and the lexical range of agrataḥ.

justifications.decision: The locative tanmātre is construed with avatiṣṭhate, “abides/remains in.” Its tad-element is anaphoric to the preceding vijñapti/vijñaptimātra. Since the verse is not otherwise listing elements, the compound is translated as “that-mere” or “mere that,” not as a Sāṃkhya-style tanmātra.
justifications.chosen: tanmātre as locative singular: “in that mere [presentation],” not “in a subtle element.”

evidence.translation: No commentary evidence supplied; decision rests on anaphora and the verb avatiṣṭhate governing a locative.

justifications.decision: avatiṣṭhate is present 3rd singular of ava-√sthā. Although middle in form, it is an intransitive kartari construction, not a passive. The negation na is fused by sandhi with the initial a- of avatiṣṭhate.

evidence.translation: No commentary evidence supplied; decision rests on the finite verb morphology and the sandhi na + avatiṣṭhate → nāvatiṣṭhate.

translation:
For even while apprehending,
“This is mere cognitive presentation (vijñaptimātra),”
if one sets some thing before oneself,
one does not abide in that mere-only (tanmātra).
one_shot_delta: Without commentary, one may wrongly take upalambhataḥ as a noun from upalambha, but the form is better accounted for as an -ant participle.
A commentary-blind reading may flatten vijñaptimātra into ordinary “mere information/request,” whereas the verse’s Yogācāra context favors “mere representation/cognitive presentation.”
tanmātre could be mistaken for the Sāṃkhya technical term “subtle element”; the local grammar favors an anaphoric “in that-mere.”
agrataḥ could be taken temporally as “first,” but sthāpayan kiñcit favors “placing something in front.”
analyzer_disagreements: ByT5 presents vijñapti + mātram with vijñapti as compound member; I record the inflected item as the single compound stem vijñaptimātra, as required for compounds.
ByT5/local_S gives evā idam, but I take the lexical words as eva + idam, with evedam as their vowel-sandhi product.

ByT5 lemmatizes upalambhataḥ as upalambha; I analyze it as genitive singular masculine of the present active participial stem upalambhat, since an a-stem noun upalambha would form genitive upalambhasya and ablative upalambhāt, not upalambhataḥ.

ByT5 gives sthāpayan under sthāpay; I retain the causative sense but, following the verifier correction, analyze the inflected word as from the present active participial stem sthāpayant, nominative masculine singular.
ByT5 gives kiñcit as kaścit; I correct the stem to neuter indefinite kiñcit, since kaścit yields the masculine form kaścit/kaścid, not neuter kiñcit.
ByT5 gives avatiṣṭhate under prefixed lemma avasthā; per the requested format I decompose it as bare root √sthā with prefix ava.
open_questions: Because the commentary is unavailable in this run, the exact doctrinal nuance of vijñapti—“representation,” “cognitive presentation,” or another school-specific gloss—cannot be checked here.

The participial construal of upalambhataḥ is grammatically strongest, but the commentary would be needed to confirm whether it was read as a genitive absolute-like concessive or as a looser genitive of the agent.
